one_time_description: Prior period adjustment: ISC transfers of $676,671 related to fiscal years 2017-2021 not previously recorded as accounts receivable
economic_notes: First Nation receives 75% of revenue from ISC (up from 52% prior year). Investments in partnerships including Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (provincial gross gaming revenues), Niiwin Wendaanimok (Four Winds Partnership), Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth LP (holds Hydro One shares), Kakina Economic Development Group LP, and Treaty #3 Investment Group. Washagamis Bay Investment Corporation operates within community.
notes: Auditor firm name not explicitly stated in document (Winnipeg, Manitoba CPA firm signed September 12, 2025 - audit report date is after FNFTA filing deadline noted as contingent liability). Prior period adjustment of $676,671 for unrecorded ISC receivables from 2017-2021. Long-term debt of $2,258,834 consists entirely of interest-free ISC loans repayable when land/flood claims are settled, with no principal repayments due until 2028. Comparative figures restated for prior period adjustment. Budget information excluded from financial statements (departure from PSAS). Two employment-related legal claims outstanding with unknown outcome. expenses_infrastructure includes Operations and Maintenance ($498,731) and Minor Capital ($1,030,189). OSR calculated as total revenue $12,303,013 less ISC $11,343,167, FNIH $439,706, Ministry of Community and Social Services $845,636, Ministry of Education $146,890, Ministry of Health and Long-Term Care $104,200, Ministry of Indigenous Affairs $0, CMHC $0, Grand Council Treaty #3 $664,758, Kenora Chiefs Advisory $370,433, Bimose Tribal Council $20,000, Niiwin Wendaanimok $200,200, Shooniyaa Wa-Biitong $102,025 = approximately $1,671,365 OSR (rental, interest, OFNLP revenue net of deferred adjustments, miscellaneous).
